steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Points drop 20% every 5 seconds, starting at 490. A 20% decrease means each interval keeps $80\%$, so each interval multiplies by $0.80$. In Desmos, make a small table: at $0$ seconds the score is $490$, at $5$ seconds it is $392$, and at $10$ seconds it is $313.6$. Exponential regression gives $a=490$ and $b=0.80$ for $y=a b^{x/5}$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$490(0.80)^{x/5}$': decay factor 0.80 per 5-second interval, exponent $x/5$ counts intervals. ✓
  • Choice B — '$490(0.20)^{x/5}$': 0.20 is the amount lost, not the amount kept — this drops to nearly zero far too fast. ✗
  • Choice C — '$490(0.80)^{5x}$': exponent $5x$ multiplies intervals by 5 instead of dividing — way too fast a decay. ✗
  • Choice D — '$490(0.20)^{5x}$': wrong factor and wrong exponent. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
